steps.title: Set up the table-testing approach
steps.content: We need all pairs in a table to satisfy $2x - y > 883$. We'll test each pair by substituting into the inequality, starting with a middle choice.

steps.title: Test choice C
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice C:
First row $(442, 0)$: $2(442) - 0 = 884 > 883$ ✓
Second row $(440, -2)$: $2(440) - (-2) = 880 + 2 = 882$. Since $882 \not> 883$, this pair fails ✗
Choice C is eliminated.

steps.title: Test choice D and confirm
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice D:
Row 1 $(442, 0)$: $2(442) - 0 = 884 > 883$ ✓
Row 2 $(441, -2)$: $2(441) - (-2) = 882 + 2 = 884 > 883$ ✓
Row 3 $(440, -4)$: $2(440) - (-4) = 880 + 4 = 884 > 883$ ✓
All three pairs satisfy the inequality. That matches choice D — D is correct.
